reaper_core.state: v7.78 (2026-07-18); this build touches ARA extensively (item-gain handling, multichannel take FX loading, save/copy/move stability, head/tail time support, channel-count dependency removal) plus API perf work on PlayTrackPreview/StopTrackPreview and midi_reinit() thread-safety.

spectralayers13.state: 13.0.10 (matches sources.yml baseline). Fixes: DePlosive threshold, Unmix Two Voices/Voice DeCrosstalk artifacts >4kHz, Ambience Match preset save, ProTools ARA/AudioSuite Bridge stability on Windows.

superpowers.state: v6.2.0, released 2026-07-23: SDD workspace is now plan-scoped (.superpowers/sdd/<plan-basename>/, deleted on clean review) fixing cross-plan ledger contamination; review-fix loop now resumes the implementer with a scoped re-review prompt and a five-round circuit breaker; branch-wide skills compression (testing-anti-patterns.md -> writing-good-tests.md with falsifiability discipline); Windows SessionStart hook now dispatches via Git Bash (fixes PowerShell/cmd.exe breakage); Gemini CLI support restored.

suno.state: Latest model: v5.5 (Voices, Custom Models, My Taste - Mar 26, 2026), still current, no newer model announced. Newest release-notes entry: Duration Slider on Web (Jul 20, 2026) - drag a duration slider in the Create form to pick song length, available on Web using V5.5. Newest blog post still: 'sad alex on songwriting, short-form, and creative autonomy' (Jul 14, 2026); pinned headline: Series D, $400M+ at $5.4B valuation (Jun 3, 2026).

inseglet.state: Windows status unchanged from baseline: compiles in CI only, NOT load-verified in a real REAPER, no tagged release. README: 'Phases 1-8 are complete and live-verified in REAPER on macOS... CI builds the macOS/Windows/Linux matrix on every push. v1 targets macOS first; Windows and Linux follow once load-verified in a real REAPER (both already compile in CI).'

mcp_spec.state: 2026-07-28 spec revision is still at Release Candidate stage (per Jun 29, 2026 'Beta SDKs for the 2026-07-28 MCP Spec Release Candidate' post) - not yet finalized. RC removes the initialize handshake and protocol-level session, adds Multi Round-Trip Requests (MRTR). Also: Enterprise-Managed Authorization (EMA) extension went stable (Jun 18, 2026).

letta_blog.state: Newest post: 'Introducing Mods: Enabling Agents to Self-Improve through Harness-Level Adaptation' (Jun 2026) - an agent-friendly way to extend/adapt the Letta Code harness. Prior: 'Introducing the Letta Code App' and 'Why Memory Isn't a Plugin' (both Apr 2026). No archival-memory/passages/blocks API changes or pricing changes noted.

letta_python.state: v1.12.1 (2026-06-02): adds generated SDK support for environment endpoints. Prior v1.12.0 (2026-05-23) and v1.11.0 (2026-05-14, cap v1 limit query params, moonshot/kimi BYOK providers). No renamed/removed routes or auth/rate-limit changes noted.
